entry.title: Foundational Elements for Harnessing the Power of Business AI in Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Ed.
entry.content:
Your boss has played with ChatGPT for the first time. He has also learned about how Generative AI is revolutionizing business processes in your industry. Now, he has come to your desk and asked when you will integrate this technology into your system landscape. He is eager to know when, in the coming weeks, he will be able to use it to address his most pressing questions. Left scratching your head, you wonder what to do next... Does this scenario sound familiar?

In this blog, I aim to provide you with some of the foundational elements necessary to harness the power of Business AI in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. This way, you'll be prepared to respond to your boss's questions with a well-thought-out plan. My intention is to give you a sense of what is possible with these technologies. I invite you to read and comment as I offer recommendations to prepare you for adopting Business AI in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.

Generative AI alone won't be able to unlock the full transformational potential in your current business operations if it isn't combined with other intelligent technologies already available in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) as a business enabler.

The true transformational value will be achieved by integrating all these elements, taking advantage of the latest advances in Generative AI and the robust foundational elements of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. These foundational elements include a stable core, which provides the right data structure to base the answers to your most pressing business questions; an excellent User Experience with SAP Fiori, which helps improve the productivity of your users; new business practices, including intelligent features like machine learning and predictive analytics, which add intelligent elements to your business processes and increase your business outcomes; and business automation, which helps free up your time so you can manage your business by exception, among others.

Before I start discussing some of the recommendations, let's first review all the features that S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides.

Please refer to the figure below.

Technologies supporting the vision of Artificial Intelligence in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

The foundation is the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Digital Core, which serves as the backbone by encompassing the correct data structure and providing out-of-the-box standard best practices. The great thing about the S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition's design is that it ensures your core remains clean by design. This makes it more easily extensible, quicker to adopt new business capabilities, and well-prepared for the upcoming wave of AI integration.

This is possible thanks to the high degree of standardization, which allows you to adopt new business functionality out of the box, including AI features, UX updates, and new best practices, twice a year during a major release upgrade.

In the past couple of years, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition has incorporated embedded intelligent technologies, such as embedded analytics, situation handling, and intelligent business scenarios (such as machine learning and predictive modeling). These technologies are now pervasive across many business processes included in the solution.  Additionally, we have recently introduced our latest digital assistant, SAP Joule, which is now planned to be embedded within the system. More on SAP Joule will be discussed later in the blog.

SAP BTP now serves as a business enabler, enhancing the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with extra services for greater competitive differentiation. These enhancements include intelligent services like SAP Build Process Automation, AI Business Services, SAP AI Core, among others.

The key to leveraging all these tools is understanding how to combine them to amplify their impact on end-to-end business processes. Therefore, let's review some recommendations I would like to offer for starting your Business AI journey with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

1. Empower your users with the Latest and Greatest SAP User Experience (UX) – Help empower your users to work more productively and have fun while doing so. I encourage you to consistently recommend the most up-to-date and advanced SAP UX to them. Suggest that they personalize their "My Home" within the SAP Horizon Theme. Additionally, if you have not yet implemented Spaces and Pages, I suggest doing so to take full advantage of the productivity enhancements they offer. Due to the higher degree of standardization in the S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Spaces and Pages are scheduled to become the default environment for all public cloud customers transitioning from the older Fiori Groups.

My Home

All these improvements are intended to help empower users and will be evident in future AI navigation systems and embedded AI business processes. 'My Home' was designed to enhance productivity by managing specific to-dos used in various scenarios, such as situation handling, tasks, favorite apps, cards, and more. This interface will offer you the best interactions with our new digital assistant SAP Joule.

SAP Joule

To Dos - Situation Handling and Tasks

Let's also not forget about the ability to use the latest Fiori apps compared with the traditional WebGUI apps offered in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Let's look at some real examples that users love in the embedded AI scenario for sales document automation (73P); this functionality is only available in the SAP Fiori app "Manage Sales Orders – Version 2" and not in the WebGUI app "Create Sales Orders – VA01." As indicated, these UX productivity improvements will play a significant role in the future of Business AI and user empowerment.

Manage Sales Orders - Version 2

Create Sales Orders - VA01

2. Consider using SAP Start if you have users utilizing different SAP Cloud Applications - If you manage multiple SAP Cloud Solutions, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Start can be an effective tool. SAP Start presents role-based content from various integrated SAP cloud business solutions. It operates on the authorization management of the integrated solutions and facilitates access through single sign-on. By consolidating these solutions into a single UX entry point, users will experience streamlined navigation that combines sophistication with simplicity. With the introduction of SAP Start, SAP has also launched SAP Joule, a new digital assistant for integrated solutions, including those mentioned in the examples above, as well as for additional SAP Cloud applications in the future.

SAP Start with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP SuccessFactors

3. Adopt the New Best Practices – Utilize SAP’s best practices, which are derived from years of intelligent data collection and design. As SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition continues to evolve, we remain committed to incorporating new best practices that are founded on intelligent processes. For instance, take the traditional Material Requirements Planning (MRP) (J44), which has been augmented with the innovative Demand Driven Buffer Level Management (1Y2) and/or Predictive Material and Resource Planning (4B5). Additionally, explore the latest scope items that integrate machine learning into their workflows or predictive scenarios that can provide extra business advantages. Examples include Machine Learning for Monitoring Goods and Invoice Receipts (2ZS) and Predictive Accounting for Sales Orders (2FD). We highly recommend that you enhance your business operations by adopting these cutting-edge  best practices.

Predictive Material and Resource Planning (4B5)

4. Start using intelligent scenarios - Take a look at the latest intelligent scenarios that have been delivered as part of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. By utilizing the new Intelligent Scenario Management Fiori app, you can gain an overview of the cutting-edge scenarios that SAP has provided out of the box. These are designed to help you make informed decisions and enhance your business processes. I recommend that you explore these scenarios and consider enabling them as necessary. In addition to the out-of-the-box scenarios, you can also create your own custom scenarios in the Intelligent Scenario Fiori app by leveraging machine learning capabilities.

Intelligent ScenariosCreate your own Intelligent Scenario

5. Automate, automate, automate! - SAP has developed SAP Build Process Automation to enhance productivity within your processes. Automation can help decrease processing times and automate tasks, enabling you to focus on managing by exception. I recommend reviewing the latest content available in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and the SAP Signavio Process Navigator regularly. Implementing automation where necessary will free your users from mundane tasks.

SAP Business Accelerator Hub

6. Make SAP Joule Your Best Friend – SAP Joule is an artificial intelligence digital assistant that truly understands your business. It will revolutionize the way you interact with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition on a day-to-day basis in a secure and compliant manner. SAP Joule is available to customers of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as part of an early adoption program, starting with release 2402. If you would like to get more details and participate, click here.

SAP Joule will be tremendously helpful in introducing generative business AI to end-users through natural language conversations. It will drive several aspects of the user experience, including, but not limited to:

  • Informational Patterns – SAP Joule will provide concise summaries of help content to effectively address user inquiries expressed in natural language.
  • Navigational Patterns – SAP Joule will recommend relevant applications and facilitate seamless navigation, empowering users to execute their business processes efficiently.
  • Transactional Patterns – Joule offers insightful information about business objects, eliminating the need to open the corresponding applications.

SAP Joule - Navigational Patterns

I recommend watching this video, which demonstrates how SAP Joule and SAP Embedded AI (Intelligent Scenario) function within the latest SAP UX in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Keep in mind that this is just the beginning; more use cases and scenarios will be enabled in the future.

7. Don't overlook the importance of human-to-human collaboration. Although this does not fall within the domain of AI (which concerns human-to-machine interactions), we are constantly improving our system's collaborative capabilities. We anticipate that, in the future, Business AI will be increasingly integrated with human collaboration efforts. Be sure to stay informed about the latest developments in this field, including the SAP Collaboration Manager (exclusively available to 3SL customers) and the enhanced collaboration features with Microsoft Teams.

SAP Collaboration ManagerTeams Collaboration "Share as Tab"Teams Collaborations "Share as Chat"

8. Review the SAP AI Roadmap – It is crucial to regularly examine the latest AI-related updates on the SAP Roadmaps page as part of your innovation planning cycle. Neglecting to do so could result in missing out on valuable opportunities.

SAP AI Roadmap

We recommend utilizing the SAP Release Assessment and Scope Dependency tool (SAP RASD) to generate tailored recommendations that align precisely with your established business processes. By doing so, you can leverage enhanced productivity and AI-driven insights to significantly improve your business outcomes planned for implementation after a release upgrade.

SAP Release Assessment and Scope Dependency

I hope these recommendations can help you find answers to the questions posed at the beginning. As indicated, Generative AI alone won't unlock the transformational potential in your current business operations if it isn't combined with other intelligent technologies. Please try to follow these recommendations to help you plan your path and provide answers to your Boss.

I also recommend listening to Episode 104 of the Inside S/4HANA Series podcast, which is scheduled to be available on March 20th, 2024. In this episode, I discuss these concepts in-depth alongside one of our customers if you're interested in diving deeper into the topic.

I would like to thank Jared Coyle, Lucas Kiesow and Bert Schulze for their review and feedback on this blog.

More Information on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, release info here
  • Product videos on our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA YouTube playlist
  • SAP S/4HANA PSCC Digital Enablement Wheel here
  • Early Release Webinar Series here

  • Feature Scope Description here
  • What’s New here
  • Help Portal Product Page here

Follow us via @SAP and #S4HANA or follow @Marco_Valencia

entry.title: Build Your SAP BTP Tech Radar with LeanIX
entry.content:
Content of an SAP BTP Tech Radar 

In many implementations of a Tech Radar, programming languages, technologies, tools, infrastructure services and other topics relating to the development and operations of IT solutions are discussed. Techniques, frameworks and approaches to modern software development are also important.

There is an outstanding catalog of services, missions and reference architectures for SAP BTP in the SAP SAP Discovery Center. However, this is not aligned and evaluated to the specific requirements of your company. Important criteria for the selection of services can be, for example, the company's own knowledge, security requirements or other solutions that are already in use. It is therefore important to evaluate these and use them to create, communicate and implement your own SAP BTP Tech Radar.

How to build Your SAP BTP Tech Radar 

A single person or a central team should not be the only responsible one for setting up your own tech radar. Rather, it is a collaborative task that experts from different teams and areas of a company should perform together. This includes not only developers from different teams, but also IT administrators, security-, business experts and architects.

This group should meet regularly to review evaluations and, if necessary, add new entries and delete those that are no longer required. The assessment made in this way should not be seen as an evaluation of individual SAP BTP solutions, but rather as a classification for your own company.

Before setting up a radar, you should first consider which areas you want to record. The following categories could be an idea for the BTP:

  • BTP Development & Tools with all services and tools required for development
  • BTP Environments & Techniques to evaluate the various runtime environments, cloud providers and techniques for development and operation
  • BTP PaaS Services for classification of the current 92 BTP platform services
  • BTP SaaS Services as a category for all SaaS solutions relating to the SAP Intelligent Enterprise, such as SAP Order Management foundation  or SAP Sales Cloud 

The rings for the evaluation - these ones could be the following:

  • Hold for things that should no longer be used in new projects
  • Incubating for solutions that may be interesting for innovative solutions or special requirements, but with which the teams have not yet gained much experience
  • Emerging for services that have already been successfully used in development and operation, but where some elements may still be missing to make them completely enterprise ready
  • Mature for things that have been tried and tested several times and are stable in operations, so that they can be used as to-go solutions for new projects

Reasons to build Your own Tech Radar

There are many reasons to carry out your own evaluation and classification of services for your company. These can be economic, legal reasons or based on the knowledge of your teams. Instead of a "centrally prescribed" list, it is a good idea to create and elaborate it together through a collaborative process. On the one hand, this usually improves the content, increases acceptance and on the other hand can be used by agile and independent teams as a starting and reference point for the realization of new projects. By feeding back the experiences from the teams, the list an be further developed by a team of architects

Benefits of LeanIX for your SAP BTP Tech Radar

A number of services used are typically already recorded in LeanIX or can be quickly added via the SaaS catalog, what can save time. The implementation in LeanIX is very simple thanks to a report available in the store. A single point of truth makes it very easy to implement and discoverable for your team members. The most important thing, however, is that this can greatly promote usage, acceptance and transparency by linking the entries to your application, processes and capabilities as well as the SAP BTP Sub Accounts and spaces where the services are used. Manual maintenance in Office solutions or a wiki is often quite time-consuming and quickly generates redundant and outdated information.

Realization in LeanIX

 

After adding the Tech Radar report and creating the tag group in the admin area, you can directly start evaluating the SAP BTP service. Simply select the values in the two tag groups for the category and the ring.

Assessment of a service

You can then check the radar directly under the reports and as well check further details on the entry and also see when the evaluation was made.

SAP BTP Tech Radar Example

Update History

Other use cases

Once you have stored and evaluated the services in LeanIX, you can easily assign them to SAP BTP Sub Accounts (Platform Fact Sheet in LeanIX) and applications and thus easily receive further informations in reports (will be described in a further blog). By maintaining your own or SAP based lifecycle information, you can also identify areas of action for you IT landscape.

In order to get the full power out of your SAP BTP instance, it is important to follow a certain level of governance and guidelines. There is a very starting point with the SAP BTP Guidance Framework.

Your ideas

What are your thoughts on this?

How do you see possible uses?

Does having your own SAP BTP Tech Radar make sense? What are the correct categories?

Who has already implemented their own Tech Radar in the SAP area and what are your experiences with it?

entry.title: Enable OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code for Sales Order (A2X) API
entry.content:
The Sales Order (A2X) on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud is one of the most utilized APIs in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and brings the efficiency of your business processes onto a new level. This API allows to create, read, update, and delete sales orders in an external system, adaptable to various authorization methods. In this blogpost we are going step-by-step through the process of enabling OAuth 2.0 with Authorization Code.

What is this about?

Setting up the Sales Order (A2X) API and enabling OAuth 2.0 is an easy thing and is well documented here. If you use the API with Basic Auth or with OAuth 2.0 with Client Credentials, the documents are created, read, updated and deleted by the technical user bound to the communication system. In most cases it is not required to identify the user if you integrate sales orders into an external system, but there are some cases where this is needed. OAuth 2.0 with Authorization Code allow API operations, which are bound to the user. 

Prerequisites

In order for OAuth 2.0 with Authorization Code to be enabled and usable, you need an application/web-server, which can provide a callback/client redirect URI. You probably have an application where you got such a callback url. In this guide I will use an API testing tool. But it should be similar in any other OData Client which supports OAuth 2.0 with Authorization Code.

Configuration on SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Determine own OAuth Endpoint Settings

Open the "Communication Systems" App and click "Own SAP Cloud System" (see Illustration 1).

Illustration 1

Here you can find the system's OAuth 2.0 endpoints. These information are needed later in the OAuth 2.0 Client (see Illustration 2).

Illustration 2

Create Communication System with Inbound Communication User

Create a new Communication System. Check "Inbound Only" in the Technical Data (General). Enter the Client Redirect URI which is obtained by the OData/OAuth client (see Illustration 3). In my case, I got this endpoint from my API testing tool. In your case, your OAuth 2.0 client library might have built in functionalities to launch a web-server, which serves as the endpoint.

Illustration 3

Maintain Users for Inbound Communication (see Illustration 4). Enter a OAuth 2.0 Client ID of your choice, this is later used in the ODATA Client.

Illustration 4

Create Communication Arrangement

Create a Communication Arrangement for the Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0109 (Sales Order Integration). Use the previously created Communication System. Review the Inbound Communication and Save (see Illustration 5).

Illustration 5

With that, you are done with the configuration on S/4HANA side. You can now switch to your ODATA Client and configure the authentication there. 

Sample Configuration of OData/OAuth-Client

In your OData Client, the following fields are important for configuration (see Illustration 6 and Illustration 7). This configuration labels might differ depending on the API testing tool you use. 

  1. Grant Type: Auth Code with PKCE.
  2. Callback Url: Authorize using browser.
  3. Auth Url: This is the address from the System Endpoints derived in the configuration Step 1, the OAuth2 Authorization Service URL ending with /sap/bc/sec/oauth2/authorize.
  4. Access Token Url: Address from the system endpoints derived in configuration step 1, OAuth 2.0 Public Client Token Service URL ending with /sap/public/bc/sec/oauth2/token.
  5. Client ID: Inbound Communication User from Step 2.
  6. Client Secret: Leave this field empty.
  7. State: Enter any string for the Oauth State, e.g. "API-Test".
  8. Client Authentication: Send Client Credentials in Body

Illustration 6

 

Illustration 7

After clicking on "Get New Access Token", the user is redirected to a browser window, where the user can authenticate with the System. After authentication, the user is asked to grant permission once. With that, the token is available for the external system and operations, which are done with the newly created token are bound to the user.

This concludes this step-by-step guide on how to enable OAuth 2.0 with Authorization Code for the Sales Order (A2X) API on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. Read further documentation and articles here:

  • SAP S/4HANA Integration Scenarios
  • APIs for Sales
  • Sales Order (A2X)

Was this article helpful for you? Please vote and share ideas and feedback for future articles in context of Sales Order Processing and Integration. Your feedback is very appreciated for future articles.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP开发案例之五：采购申请批量上传
entry.content:
紧随这篇博客，我们将再次逐步详细地介绍一个利用SAP S/4HANA Cloud三系统来做开发扩展的案例。

 

1. 案例背景:

在这个案例中，我们会创建一个自定义的Fiori应用程序，通过该程序，我们可以从Excel文件批量创建多个采购申请记录。同时，该Excel导入模板可以根据我们的需求不同而完全自定义。

2. 后台服务开发

2.1 创建数据库表

为了保存从Excel文件上传的数据，我们首先创建一个数据库表：

定义一个表来保存Excel上传的数据

 

2.2 创建CDS数据模型

然后我们在数据库表之上创建CDS视图，作为与数据库交互的第一层。通过Annotations为所需字段配置不同的功能。

然后，我们在上一步创建的CDS视图之上再创建一个投影视图（projection view），作为一个独立的功能层，我们可以过滤特定的字段。

2.3 创建行为定义

 

然后创建行为实现类，当用户点击“Create Purchase Requisition”按钮时，将触发事件CREATE_PR，并通过EML创建采购申请。

 

2.4 创建服务定义和服务绑定

右键单击投影视图，接下来我们将创建一个新的服务定义。我们可以选择哪个实体将暴露于服务。由于我们只有一个实体，所以我们使用系统生成的代码。

 

然后右键单击服务定义来创建服务绑定：

 

在发布之后，我们可以预览该服务如下：

 

3. 前台应用开发

打开SAP Business Application Studio服务：

 

选择SAP Fiori Worklist Application：

选择我们之前创建的服务：

 

如下是该应用的文件结构：

对于Excel上传功能组件，可以参考以下博客：

Excel Upload using RAP: Part -1 | SAP Blogs

 

值得一提的是，我们在SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation Store上有一个预交付的包，它也可以从本地Excel文件创建采购申请。详细链接如下：

Create Purchase Requisitions from Excel (48M)

然而，这两种方法的技术实现是不同的，在我们的例子中，我们使用的业务对象接口仅用于开发人员扩展性；对于iRPA，它使用公开发布的API。我们不能说一种方法比另一种更好，我们必须根据我们的情况选择合适的方法。

 

以上是该案例——采购申请批量创建自开发应用的步骤介绍。希望通过此案例的介绍能让您了解到三系统开发能实现的常见功能。

entry.title: How to Choose the Right IT Contact for Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Project
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a powerful and innovative solution that can help you transform your business and achieve your goals. However, to ensure a smooth and successful implementation, you need to have the right IT contact on board from the start. This is the person who will be responsible for setting up the initial system access, provisioning users, and managing authentication. Alongside the Communication contacts this person will also be the recipient of all ongoing SAP communications and notifications, so they need to be available and responsive throughout the project.

 

Unfortunately, some customers make the mistake of choosing an IT contact who is not suitable for this role. For example, they might name a high-level executive, such as the CFO, who is too busy or not technically savvy enough to handle the system administration tasks. Or they might name someone who is not easily reachable or who has other priorities that prevent them from dedicating enough time and attention to the project. This can lead to serious delays and complications that can jeopardize the project timeline and quality.

 

To avoid these issues, here are some tips on how to choose the right IT contact for your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project:

  • Choose someone who is a hands-on system administrator, not an executive. This person should have the skills and experience to perform the system setup and configuration tasks, as well as troubleshoot any issues that might arise.
  • Choose someone who is available and accessible throughout the project. They should also be able to respond to SAP communications and notifications promptly and effectively.
  • Choose someone who is committed and engaged with the project. This person should be willing to collaborate with the project team, provide feedback, and participate in testing and training activities. They should also be able to communicate with the business stakeholders and align the system requirements with the business goals.

 

By choosing the right IT contact for your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project, you can ensure a faster and smoother implementation, and avoid unnecessary delays and complications.

entry.title: Professional Services in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - The Collection
entry.content:
Professional Services in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition supports professional services companies with the digital transformation of their company. With this solution we deliver real-time accounting solutions with in-depth analysis, the right functionality to plan, execute and control profitable projects, the insight to deploy the right resources to the right project at the right time, and sophisticated billing capabilities with advanced revenue recognition.

In this blog, you can find a collection of expert blog posts that relate to Professional Services in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and give you some details about the end-to-end processes of this scenario.

This is a new version, of this existing blog. Due to the migration of the SAP Community, updates will be made here in the future.
